summary.note_sub_zones: Topline as of 2026-06-01 (DRC MoH / Africa CDC, via ECDC and NPR): DRC 282 confirmed (42 deaths) — Ituri 264 across 14 health zones, North Kivu 15, South Kivu 3; Uganda 9 confirmed (1 death) — combined 291 confirmed and 43 confirmed deaths. Cumulative suspected passed 1,100 (246 suspected deaths); ECDC separately lists 220 cases actively under investigation. Confirmed cases more than doubled from 134 on 2026-05-29 as the diagnostic backlog was processed. The province- and health-zone-level breakdown below remains at earlier attribution pending the next detailed MoH bulletin with health-zone splits.

regions.name: Mongbwalu HZ
regions.notes: First cases detected here. Index case was a healthcare worker.

regions.name: Rwampara HZ
regions.notes: Six health areas affected. Transmission chains unclear.

regions.name: Bunia
regions.notes: Index case died here. Provincial coordination center.

regions.name: North Kivu (Goma + Butembo)
regions.notes: ECDC (2026-05-19): 4 confirmed across Goma and Butembo (newly affected); 2 deaths confirmed. Goma index case traveled from Ituri; Butembo cluster identified 2026-05-18.

regions.name: Kampala
regions.notes: Seven lab-confirmed cases among travelers from DRC and contacts. One death. Three new cases announced May 23: a driver who transported the first patient, a healthcare worker, and a woman who traveled from DRC.

regions.name: Kinshasa
regions.notes: One confirmed case in the capital, ~1,000 km from Ituri epicenter. Reported May 17-18. Status flagged for re-validation: subsequent secondary reporting suggests the initial confirmation may have been a false positive — awaiting Ministry of Health re-test.

regions.name: Bukavu (South Kivu)
regions.notes: First confirmed case in South Kivu (2026-05-21). Patient traveled from Kisangani (Tshopo Province); died before diagnosis confirmed. Territory under M23 control — surveillance and contact tracing severely constrained. Source: Al Jazeera, Jamaica Observer (2026-05-21).

at_risk.name: South Sudan
at_risk.notes: Shares border with Ituri Province. Flagged by Africa CDC.

at_risk.name: Rwanda
at_risk.notes: Closed Rubavu-Goma border crossing May 18 (indefinite). Only DRC/Rwandan nationals returning home permitted. Unilateral — against WHO advice.

timeline.note: ECDC methodology revision reduced suspected death attribution
DRC MoH 2026-05-26 report via ECDC: 121 confirmed + 1,077 suspected; Uganda 7 confirmed. Suspected-death attribution reconciled upward.
WHO DON605: DRC revised figures on 2026-05-28 (removed non-cases, reclassified some suspected as confirmed); suspected cases and deaths fell while confirmed rose.

DRC MoH figures as of 2026-05-31 (Africa CDC, NPR, ECDC): DRC 282 confirmed (42 deaths) across Ituri (264, 14 health zones), North Kivu (15) and South Kivu (3); Uganda 9 confirmed (1 death). Confirmed cases more than doubled as the diagnostic backlog cleared; cumulative suspected passed 1,100 with 246 suspected deaths.
